name: International-Student I-20 / Visa Issue with the DSO
description: Call the international-students office's Designated School Official (DSO) to handle an I-20 / SEVIS / F-1 (or J-1) status issue: late arrival, reduced course load, change of major or program level, employment authorization (CPT / OPT), travel signature, transfer-in / transfer-out, dependent I-20s, and status reinstatement after a violation.
disclaimer: Immigration status is governed by federal regulations (8 CFR 214.2(f) / (j) for F-1 / J-1, and SEVP / DOS policy). The DSO is the ONLY person authorized to update SEVIS for an F-1; the Responsible Officer (RO/ARO) does so for J-1. Nothing in this skill is legal advice; complex cases (visa fraud accusations, reinstatement denials, immigrant intent disputes) warrant an immigration attorney.

context.when_to_use: User is on F-1 or J-1 status and needs to (a) get a travel signature on the I-20 / DS-2019, (b) authorize Curricular Practical Training or apply for Optional Practical Training, (c) drop below full course load (RCL — Reduced Course Load) for medical, academic, or final-term reasons, (d) change major / program level / education level, (e) transfer SEVIS record to another school, (f) add dependents (F-2 / J-2), (g) request reinstatement after a status violation, or (h) handle visa-stamp / port-of-entry issues for re-entry.
preconditions: User has SEVIS ID (starts with N00…), passport, I-20 or DS-2019, visa stamp, and most recent I-94 (download from CBP).
User knows the specific issue, including dates (start of program, last entry, current term dates).
User has checked the international-students office's website for the request form (most issues have a specific form: travel signature request, RCL request, CPT request, OPT request, etc.).

principles: Only the DSO can touch SEVIS. Not the registrar, not the dean, not the professor. If front-desk staff is making promises about SEVIS, slow down and confirm with a DSO.
Timelines are FEDERAL, not institutional. Late arrival, dropping below full-time, working without authorization — these are status violations that the school cannot waive.
RCL (Reduced Course Load) is the safe way to drop below 12 credits (undergrad) or 9 credits (graduate). Without an authorized RCL, dropping below is a status violation.
CPT must be authorized BEFORE the user starts work, on the specific employer and specific dates. Working a day early is a violation.
OPT applications take USCIS 3–5 months. STEM OPT extension applications are time-sensitive. Plan months ahead.
Travel signatures (page 2 of the I-20 / DS-2019) are valid for one year (six months while on OPT). Get a fresh one BEFORE every international trip.
Reinstatement is possible for some violations but requires fast action and immigration-attorney involvement for anything beyond minor lapses.

phrases.opener_general: Hi, I'm an F-1 / J-1 student, SEVIS ID [N00…]. I have a question about [travel signature / RCL / CPT / OPT / transfer / dependent / reinstatement]. Could I speak with a DSO?
phrases.ask_for_DSO_directly: I'd like to speak with a Designated School Official, not just front-desk staff — this is an immigration question and I want to make sure the answer is authoritative.
phrases.travel_signature_request: I'm planning international travel on [dates]. Could I get a travel signature added to page 2 of my I-20 / DS-2019? What's the process — drop-off, email, in-person?
phrases.RCL_request: I need to drop below full-time enrollment this term because [medical / academic difficulty in first term / final term needs fewer credits]. Could you process an RCL authorization in SEVIS before I drop the course?
phrases.CPT_request: I have a CPT internship offer from [employer]. The dates are [start-end], the hours are [N]/week, and the work is integral to my [program]. What does your office need to issue a CPT authorization on my I-20?
phrases.OPT_inquiry: I'm planning to apply for post-completion OPT. My program ends [date]. When's the earliest I can file the I-765, and what does your office need to issue the OPT-recommended I-20?
phrases.STEM_OPT_inquiry: I'm currently on OPT and want to apply for the 24-month STEM extension. My current OPT ends [date]. Could we map the timeline and form requirements?
phrases.transfer_in_or_out: I'm transferring [in from / out to] [school] for [term]. Could we schedule the SEVIS transfer release / transfer-in pickup?
phrases.reinstatement: I may have fallen out of status because [reason]. I need urgent guidance — possibly reinstatement, possibly a travel-and-reentry option. Can we get on the calendar today?
phrases.ask_for_director_of_OISS: Could I escalate to the Director of International Student Services? My situation is complex and I want to make sure I'm being routed correctly.
phrases.graceful_close_success: Thank you. To confirm: [form] submitted by [date], expected I-20 issuance [date], pickup method [in-person / mail / e-sign]. May I have your direct email as my DSO contact?

tactics.name: Get to a DSO on the first call
tactics.script: Use `ask_for_DSO_directly`. Front-desk staff can answer logistics; only DSOs can answer the actual immigration question. Avoid building a plan on advice that has to be re-confirmed later.

tactics.name: Authorize BEFORE doing
tactics.script: Every immigration action requires authorization in SEVIS or on the I-20 before the action. Working before CPT issuance, dropping before RCL authorization, traveling without a current signature — all are violations even when 'fixed' afterward. Use `CPT_request`, `RCL_request`, `travel_signature_request` proactively.

tactics.name: Plan OPT timing backward from program-end date
tactics.script: Use `OPT_inquiry`. I-765 can be filed up to 90 days before program end and up to 60 days after. The OPT-recommended I-20 must be issued first. Total process: schedule a DSO meeting 4 months out.

tactics.name: Handle transfer SEVIS releases carefully
tactics.script: Use `transfer_in_or_out`. The release date determines which school controls SEVIS. Don't release too early (you can lose access to the old campus systems) or too late (new school can't issue the I-20).

tactics.name: Treat potential status violations as same-day
tactics.when: User suspects a violation (overstayed term, unauthorized work, dropped below full-time without RCL, expired I-20).
tactics.script: Use `reinstatement`. Time is critical: reinstatement applications generally must be filed within 5 months of the violation, and the 'travel and reentry' option may not work after that. Get the DSO involved today, then loop in an immigration attorney.

tactics.name: Escalate to the OISS Director for non-standard cases
tactics.when: Complex transfers, dependent issues, denied OPT, change-of-status involvement.
tactics.script: Use `ask_for_director_of_OISS`. Directors handle exceptions and have direct lines to SEVP / SEVIS-help. They also know which immigration attorneys the school's students have used.

tactics.name: Document with photos, not staples
tactics.script: Photograph both pages. Keep every prior I-20 forever — OPT, H-1B, green-card adjudicators may ask for any of them years later. Don't staple or write on the I-20.

boundaries: Do NOT work without authorization, even one hour, even unpaid 'volunteer' work in a paid role.
Do NOT drop below full-time without an authorized RCL in SEVIS.
Do NOT travel internationally without a current travel signature.
Do NOT misrepresent the funding or employment on the I-20 — it can affect future visa adjudications.
Do NOT take immigration advice from non-DSO staff, faculty, or friends as authoritative. Confirm with the DSO.
success_signals: DSO confirms the action in SEVIS (RCL authorized, CPT printed on I-20, OPT-recommended I-20 issued).
Travel signature dated within the last 6 months.
Form receipt or pickup date scheduled.
DSO offers direct email and named follow-up.
Transfer release / pickup dates are aligned with the new school's start.
failure_signals: Violation already occurred and reinstatement window is closing.
CPT request denied because the work isn't integral to the program.
Dependent I-20 can't be issued without additional financial documentation.
Transfer-out school disputes the release date.

exit_strategy.on_success: Confirm SEVIS action, I-20 / DS-2019 issuance date, pickup method, DSO email. Photograph the new document immediately.
exit_strategy.on_failure: Stop. Do not act on a violation track without the DSO + ideally an immigration attorney. Identify whether reinstatement, travel-and-reentry, or change-of-status is the path. Loop the operator immediately.
follow_ups: Email operator the new I-20 / DS-2019 dates and DSO contact.
If OPT: calendar I-765 receipt notice, EAD card arrival, employer reporting deadlines, and unemployment day-count.
If reinstatement: calendar all filing windows and attorney consult.

required_user_info: SEVIS ID, passport number, visa-stamp expiration, I-94 admit-until date
Current I-20 / DS-2019 with travel signature date
Program major, level (BS/MS/PhD), current term, expected end date
Specific issue (travel, RCL, CPT, OPT, STEM OPT, transfer, dependent, reinstatement)
Funding source and amount on the I-20
History of prior status changes (CPT used, prior OPT, prior schools)
